original_registry_entry.issuing_organisation: American Academy of Otolaryngology–Head and Neck Surgery Foundation
original_registry_entry.exact_document_title: Clinical Practice Guideline: Adult Sinusitis Update
original_registry_entry.jurisdiction: United States specialty guideline; international evidence requiring UAE adult ENT, imaging, prescribing, and referral adaptation
original_registry_entry.population: Adults with acute or chronic rhinosinusitis; the official web-page target-age wording is internally inconsistent and is retained as an applicability limitation.
original_registry_entry.clinical_setting: Primary, urgent, outpatient, and ENT assessment and review of adult rhinosinusitis.
original_registry_entry.applicability_note: Exact for adult acute-sinusitis symptom pattern, reassessment, chronic-rhinosinusitis objective confirmation, modifying factors, polyps, and clinician-reviewed allergy or immune testing. It does not diagnose bacterial infection, recommend antibiotics, or order imaging.

exact_sections.heading: Key action statements 1A–1B — acute rhinosinusitis distinction and clinical diagnosis context
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports documenting symptom duration and pattern, purulent drainage, nasal obstruction, facial pain or pressure, worsening pattern, associated symptoms, and relevant negatives without asserting viral or bacterial diagnosis.

exact_sections.heading: Key action statement 6 — treatment-failure or worsening reassessment
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports interval change, persistent or worsening symptom review, clinician reassessment, and documented follow-up without changing treatment.

exact_sections.heading: Key action statements 7A–10 — chronic distinction, objective confirmation, modifiers, testing, and polyps
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports documenting chronic or recurrent pattern, clinician-performed nasal examination, already available endoscopy or CT, asthma, cystic fibrosis, immunocompromise, ciliary dyskinesia, allergy or immune-testing context, and presence or absence of polyps without generating testing or treatment.

title: Clinical Practice Guideline: Adult Sinusitis Update
publisher: American Academy of Otolaryngology–Head and Neck Surgery Foundation
jurisdiction: United States specialty guideline; international evidence requiring UAE adult ENT, imaging, prescribing, and referral adaptation
population: Adults with acute or chronic rhinosinusitis; the official web-page target-age wording is internally inconsistent and is retained as an applicability limitation.
intended_setting: Primary, urgent, outpatient, and ENT assessment and review of adult rhinosinusitis.
